name: Empires
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Seedless raspberry jam or Currant jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the butter and granulated sugar in a large bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Gradually add the flour and mix well. Remove from the bowl and knead until smooth. Divide the dough into two; wrap each piece in wax paper. Refrigerate the dough until firm enough to roll out, several hours or longer. If the dough is too firm to roll, let it soften slightly on a counter.
2. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit. Roll out the chilled dough between two sheets of floured wax paper or flatten it with floured hands to a thickness of 1/4 inch on a floured surface. Use cookie cutters to cut shapes. Transfer the cookies to ungreased baking sheets. Bake for only until slightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Allow the cookies to cool on the baking sheets just long enough to firm up, then transfer them to wire racks to cool completely.
3. To assemble, spread a small amount of jam between two cookies and sandwich them together. In a small bowl, mix the confectioners' sugar with milk until it forms a thin glaze. Frost the assembled cookies lightly with the glaze and sprinkle with colored sugar. Allow the glaze to set.
This delicate cookie, created by Keith and Teresa Duncan of Batavia, won third place in the Chicago Tribune's sixth annual Food Guide Holiday Cookie Contest on December 2, 1993.
